title: The War of the Worlds - Curriculum Unit Card 👽

notes: Launch-ready Grade 8 card with reading, vocabulary, journals, writing, questions, informational text, and assessments.
Structured from existing markdown unit card.

book.title: The War of the Worlds - Curriculum Unit Card 👽
book.description: Grade Level: 8th Grade (adaptable to 7th-9th) Duration: 15 days (12 reading days + 3 assessments) Genre: Science Fiction / Invasion Narrative Theme: Survival, Human Vulnerability, Adaptation Under Pressure Curriculum Days: 16-30 (Quarter 1, Weeks 4-6)

weeks.title: Week 4: The War of the Worlds - Curriculum Unit Card 👽 Part 1 (Days 16-20)
reading: Chapters 1-8: The War of the Worlds - Curriculum Unit Card 👽
vocabulary: Invasion: define, use in context, and connect to the text
literary evidence: embed and explain a quotation
academic vocabulary: analyze author craft and theme
journal: What does this section reveal about Invasion? Use one quotation or scene as evidence.
Which character choice, conflict, or image feels most important so far? Explain why.
writing: Write an analytical paragraph about setting, conflict, or character introduction.
Strengthen one response by adding a precise quotation and commentary.
questions: What conflict or question is driving this section of the book?
How does the author use setting, mood, structure, or point of view?
Which quotation best supports your interpretation of a character or theme?
How does this section connect to the larger quarter theme?
assessment: Weekly comprehension check, vocabulary application, and short analytical response.
Review day: discussion, revision, and evidence check.

weeks.title: Week 5: The War of the Worlds - Curriculum Unit Card 👽 Part 2 (Days 21-25)
reading: Chapters 9-16: The War of the Worlds - Curriculum Unit Card 👽
vocabulary: Technology: define, use in context, and connect to the text
literary evidence: embed and explain a quotation
academic vocabulary: analyze author craft and theme
journal: What does this section reveal about Technology? Use one quotation or scene as evidence.
Which character choice, conflict, or image feels most important so far? Explain why.
writing: Write a comparison paragraph connecting the fiction to the paired informational text.
Strengthen one response by adding a precise quotation and commentary.
questions: What conflict or question is driving this section of the book?
How does the author use setting, mood, structure, or point of view?
Which quotation best supports your interpretation of a character or theme?
How does this section connect to the larger quarter theme?
assessment: Weekly comprehension check, vocabulary application, and short analytical response.
Review day: discussion, revision, and evidence check.

weeks.title: Week 6: The War of the Worlds - Curriculum Unit Card 👽 Part 3 (Days 26-30)
reading: Chapters 17-24: The War of the Worlds - Curriculum Unit Card 👽
vocabulary: Human vulnerability: define, use in context, and connect to the text
literary evidence: embed and explain a quotation
academic vocabulary: analyze author craft and theme
journal: What does this section reveal about Human vulnerability? Use one quotation or scene as evidence.
Which character choice, conflict, or image feels most important so far? Explain why.
writing: Write a short literary analysis paragraph about theme, author craft, or moral complexity.
Revise one paragraph into the end-of-card portfolio response.
questions: What conflict or question is driving this section of the book?
How does the author use setting, mood, structure, or point of view?
Which quotation best supports your interpretation of a character or theme?
How does this section connect to the larger quarter theme?
assessment: Weekly comprehension check, vocabulary application, and short analytical response.
Book-card assessment: passage analysis, vocabulary, and literary analysis paragraph.
